name: Labuan (Malaysia)

colors.symbolism: the sovereignty of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong, on the crescent and star

description: Three horizontal bands of red, white, and blue, with a yellow crescent and a yellow fourteen-pointed federal star (Bintang Persekutuan) in the centre.
history: Labuan, an island off the coast of Borneo, became a Federal Territory of Malaysia on 16 April 1984. Its flag, adopted on 31 August 1992, uses three horizontal bands of red, white and blue charged with the crescent and fourteen-pointed federal star of Malaysia, marking its federal-territory status. Its main town is Victoria.

features.symbolism: Islam, the official religion of Malaysia
the unity of the states and federal territories of Malaysia
